headline: Inside the fight over Claude Mythos 5
summary: Anthropic faced a US export control directive for its Mythos 5 and Fable 5 AI models. The Trump administration ordered the suspension of access to these latest models. Anthropic spent the weekend fighting the government directive.

headline: Facebook's new AI Mode search gets its info from public posts
summary: Meta's new "AI Mode" on Facebook will leverage public posts for AI-generated search results. The feature appears alongside existing search options like "People" and "Marketplace." This is part of a broader rollout of new AI capabilities, including photo presets.

headline: Xbox is closing down Hellblade creator Ninja Theory
summary: Xbox is reportedly closing Ninja Theory, the acclaimed studio behind the Hellblade series. Staff were informed of the closure on Monday, but hope for a potential buyer. This comes amidst similar closures affecting other Xbox studios like Compulsion Games.

headline: Fox wants to take over your TV — and the tech inside it
summary: Fox is acquiring streaming hardware company Roku in a $22 billion deal. The acquisition aims to extend Fox's reach to over 100 million homes globally. Roku's user interface is not expected to change immediately post-acquisition.

headline: Obsession Director Curry Barker Says Gen Z Is 'Tired of Slop' and 'Will Show Up' for Good Movies
summary: Director Curry Barker claims Gen Z will flock to theaters for quality films. He states younger audiences are "tired of slop" in the movie industry. Barker emphasizes that good movies are essential to attract Gen Z viewers.

headline: Harry Potter TV Series Casts Peter Serafinowicz in Role Not Featured in the Films
summary: Peter Serafinowicz has been cast as Peeves the Poltergeist in the HBO Harry Potter series. This character was notably absent from the original film adaptations. Peeves will make his live-action debut in the upcoming television show.

headline: Grab an Alienware RTX 5080 Gaming PC With 32GB of RAM and 2TB SSD for $2,579 Before It Sells Out
summary: An Alienware RTX 5080 Gaming PC is on sale for $2,579 at Dell Outlet. The powerful rig includes 32GB of RAM and a spacious 2TB SSD. It comes with a full 1-year warranty, matching new purchases from Dell.com.

headline: All of the Dungeon Crawler Carl Books Are on Sale at Amazon Before Prime Day
summary: The popular Dungeon Crawler Carl book series is currently on sale at Amazon. This special promotion is running in anticipation of Prime Day. Fans can grab the entire series at a discounted price.

headline: Trump may release US-Iran deal before Friday, Vance says
summary: Vice-President Vance suggests a US-Iran deal might be unveiled before Friday. The agreement is described as concise, spanning "about a page and a half." Many specific details are expected to be finalized after its initial release.

headline: Russia was behind arson attacks targeting PM, BBC reveals
summary: BBC investigation reveals Russia orchestrated arson attacks targeting a Prime Minister. Evidence indicates Russian operatives directed the plots. They also exploited fake far-right and Muslim groups to escalate tensions.

headline: Thames Water moves step closer to nationalisation after government objects to rescue deal
summary: Thames Water is nearing nationalisation after the government rejected its rescue plan. The government deemed the current offer insufficient to protect consumers or the environment. This objection brings the utility company closer to state control.

headline: Married at First Sight Australia allegations 'disturbing', says country's watchdog
summary: Australia's media watchdog labeled allegations from Married at First Sight Australia as "disturbing." The claims also prompted "deep concern" from UK media regulator Ofcom. Both regulators are scrutinizing the concerning allegations made about the show.
